title: The Plateau Without Return

features.name: woven edge
features.desc-on-touch: the plateau's boundary is not a line but a pattern — threads of rust and gold that don't end, they tessellate into the next distance

features.name: colour grid
features.desc-on-touch: a survey marker or a textile loom's measuring frame — each square holds a shade that means something in the archive's silent language: rotation, depth, time stopped

features.name: horizon marker
features.desc-on-touch: a stake or cairn or the memory of one, marking the point where the spiral turned outward and the observer became observed — you are inside the pattern now

features.name: pattern memory
features.desc-on-touch: red and gold threads arranged as if a hand had left them moments ago, still holding the shape of a deliberate meaning — if you follow them backward, they lead only forward

features.name: standing view
features.desc-on-touch: from here the plateau extends in all directions, and you are the still point it was always describing — the landscape and you have become the same proof

inscription: the spiral continues outward until it is measuring you; here you are the landscape it was always describing

file.name: Textile_Survey_Grid_Plateau_Coordinates.txt
file.note: a weaver's notation system applied to the land: each colour in the plateau corresponds to a thread count, a turning, a moment of the observer's position in the spiral

exits.label: back down the ridge toward the spiral's memory
into the horizon where the landscape doesn't return
across the woven boundary toward the far country
